exercises.title: Error Accumulation Simulator
exercises.description: Build a simulator comparing coherent vs incoherent error accumulation.
tasks: Implement coherent over-rotation errors
Implement incoherent depolarizing errors
Apply n gates with each error type
Track fidelity vs number of gates
Verify n vs √n scaling behavior
hints: Coherent: apply R_z(ε) n times, total phase = n×ε
Incoherent: infidelity ≈ n × p where p = ε²/4
Use |+⟩ state to see phase errors clearly
Plot log(infidelity) vs log(n) to verify power law

exercises.title: Randomized Compiling Implementation
exercises.description: Implement randomized compiling for single-qubit Clifford gates.
tasks: Create Pauli twirl for single-qubit gates
Compute the inverse Pauli for each gate
Verify twirled circuits are logically equivalent
Show that twirling converts coherent to depolarizing errors
Compare fidelity with and without twirling over many gates
exercises.expectedOutput: Working randomized compiler with error reduction demonstration
hints: For Clifford C: C·P·C† is another Pauli
Use lookup table for Clifford-Pauli conjugation
Average over many random Pauli choices
Twirled channel should approach depolarizing form

exercises.title: Coherent Error Source Modeler
exercises.description: Model realistic sources of coherent errors in superconducting qubits.
tasks: Model pulse amplitude miscalibration
Model frequency drift over circuit execution
Model ZZ crosstalk between neighboring qubits
Calculate accumulated errors for realistic circuit depths
Identify which error source dominates
hints: Sum coherent errors (they add, not subtract randomly)

exercises.title: Composite Pulse Designer
exercises.description: Implement composite pulse sequences to suppress coherent errors.
tasks: Implement BB1 composite pulse sequence
Verify first-order error cancellation
Compare single pulse vs BB1 for various error magnitudes
Extend to SK1 sequence
Analyze trade-off: more pulses vs error suppression
exercises.expectedOutput: Composite pulse library with error suppression verification
hints: BB1 for θ rotation: θ_π - 2θ_φ - θ_π - 2θ_-φ where φ = arccos(-θ/4π)
More pulses = more time = more decoherence
Optimal only when coherent errors dominate
